# MakerSquare

> MakerSquare is a 2-week, in-person AI intensive in Austin, TX for non-technical professionals: operators, founders, career builders, and domain experts. No coding required. Students build and deploy 3 real AI products and present at Demo Day in front of Austin's tech, investor, and operator community.

## What MakerSquare Is

MakerSquare is the only in-person AI product-building intensive in Austin, Texas designed specifically for professionals without a technical background. Unlike online courses or certifications that teach AI literacy, MakerSquare graduates ship working AI products. The program is 2 weeks long, meets Monday through Friday from 9am to 3pm, and is intentionally kept to 15 builders per cohort. Optional Saturday office hours are offered for extra build time.

The program is open to anyone nationwide (and internationally), not just Austin locals. Housing assistance is available for builders who relocate temporarily.

## Who It's For

- Business operators who want to automate their workflows
- Career professionals who want to future-proof their skills with real AI building experience
- Domain experts with industry knowledge and a specific problem to solve
- First-time founders who want to build a product without hiring a developer

## What Students Build

Over 2 weeks (10 teaching days), students build and deploy 3 AI products:
1. Product 1 (Day 5, Friday of Week 1): Website + Supabase + Claude Skill, wired together and deployed to a live URL.
2. Product 2 (Day 9, Thursday of Week 2): A workflow or automation combining Composio, Playwright, or voice AI with the builder's real data.
3. Capstone (Day 10, Demo Day Friday): Most ambitious product combining Claude Code, agentic APIs, and the full stack. Presented at public Demo Day.

## Curriculum Overview

- Pre-work: 3 self-paced steps (set up accounts, run your first expert-level conversation with Claude, define your capstone problem)
- Week 1: Build. How models work, talking to Claude like an expert, Warp + GitHub + Obsidian, PRD writing, build and deploy a website with Lovable + Vercel, Supabase and Claude Skills. Product 1 ships Friday.
- Week 2: Automate + Ship. Sales automation with Composio, Playwright browser control, voice AI with Whisper + ElevenLabs, Claude Code + CodeRabbit agentic workflows, advanced tools. Demo Day Friday.

No visual automation tools (Zapier, Make, n8n). Production-level skills: expert prompting, Claude Skills, agentic workflows, browser control, voice AI, real deployment.

## Tools & Tech Stack

Claude, Claude Code, ChatGPT, OpenRouter, Warp Terminal, GitHub, Vercel, Supabase, Lovable, Composio, Playwright, CodeRabbit, Obsidian, Splashtop, ElevenLabs, Whisper

## Part-Time Format (Coming Soon)

A 4-week part-time format (Fridays plus weekends) is planned as a separate track for professionals who can't take two full weeks away from work. Same curriculum, same three shipped products, same public Demo Day. Waitlist open on the curriculum page.

## Key Facts

- Duration: 2 weeks (10 teaching days, Mon through Fri)
- Format: In-person, Austin TX (Mon-Fri, 9am to 3pm), optional Saturday office hours
- Prerequisites: No coding required. Basic computer fluency plus real-world domain expertise.
- Cohort size: 15 builders per cohort
- Tuition: $5,999 (flat fee, no ISA)
- Payment plans: Pay in full $5,999 or 2 payments of $3,100 ($6,199 total)
- Deposit: $1,000 locks seat
- Scholarship: $1,500 off for military, first responders, and educators ($4,499 total)
- Housing: Partner Airbnb discounts from $1,600/month
- Corporate training: custom 2-week intensive for teams of 8 to 20 (custom pricing)
- Alumni subscription: $199/month (optional, first month free)

## 2026 Cohort Calendar (2-Week Intensive)

- Cohort 1: July 6 to July 17, 2026 (Demo Day Fri July 17 evening) — 15 seats
- Cohort 2: July 27 to August 7, 2026 (Demo Day Fri August 7 evening) — 15 seats
- Cohort 3: August 17 to August 28, 2026 (Demo Day Fri August 28 evening) — 15 seats
- Cohort 4: September 8 to September 18, 2026 (Demo Day Fri September 18 evening) — 15 seats. Starts Tuesday due to Labor Day (Mon Sep 7); 9 teaching days. Saturday office hours remain optional drop-in, not a required makeup class.
- Cohort 5: September 28 to October 9, 2026 (Demo Day Fri October 9 evening) — 15 seats
- Cohort 6: October 19 to October 30, 2026 (Demo Day Fri October 30 evening) — 15 seats
- Cohort 7: November 9 to November 20, 2026 (Demo Day Fri November 20 evening) — 15 seats. Veterans Day (Nov 11) is a day off mid-program.
- Cohort 8: November 30 to December 11, 2026 (Demo Day Fri December 11 evening) — 15 seats. Starts after the Thanksgiving break. Ends before Christmas.

Cohorts run Monday through Friday, 9am to 3pm, with optional Saturday office hours (10am to 1pm).

## Demo Day

Every cohort ends with a public Demo Day at MakerSquare HQ in Austin. Each builder presents their capstone AI product to an audience of seed-stage investors, Austin tech hub leaders, and operators. 5-minute live demos with real feedback from people who build and invest.

## Why AI Skills Matter Now

88% of organizations use AI in at least one business function, but only 1% have achieved real AI maturity (McKinsey, 2025). Jobs requiring AI fluency have grown 7x in two years (Gloat, 2026). 59% of enterprise leaders report an AI skills gap (DataCamp, 2026). The gap between people who prompt AI and people who build with AI is widening every month.

## Differentiators

- The only in-person AI product-building intensive for non-technical professionals in Austin
- Builds real, deployed products, not coursework or certificates
- Graduates ship 3 AI products and present at Demo Day to Austin investors and operators
- No visual automation tools. Teaches production-level AI building with Claude Code, Claude Skills, Composio, Playwright, APIs, and agentic workflows
- 2-week intensive format: fully immersive, Mon-Fri, 9am to 3pm, with optional Saturday office hours
- 4.41/5 alumni rating on Course Report (legacy MakerSquare program)

## Founder

Marliis Schneider, Founder & CEO. Background in operations, business development, and startup ecosystems. Built MakerSquare after seeing the gap between professionals who know what AI is and those who know how to make it work. Based in Austin, TX.

## Advisory Board

Ravi Parikh — Co-founder & CEO, Heap (YC alum). Additional advisors from Austin's operator, investor, and AI community being assembled.
